entries.description: Read spec.md for set 004 and identified Session 1 as next: dual-sourced print_cost_report with discrepancy flagging, --format json, backward-compatible get_costs, and required test scenarios. User instructed: no external routing this session except cross-provider verification, with Gemini Pro as verifier.
Registered session-state.json for session 1 with orchestrator metadata.

Moved get_costs() and print_cost_report() out of ai-router/__init__.py into a new module ai-router/cost_report.py, re-exported from __init__.py. Reason: __init__.py has transitive API-key-dependent imports (verifier_role, orchestrator_role, providers, etc.) so unit tests cannot import from the package without setting API keys; cost_report.py is a leaf module that can be imported under the test conftest's sys.path entry.

Implemented _load_routed_metrics_for_session_set() that reads ai-router/router-metrics.jsonl filtered by session_set (canonical billing-grade), aggregating total_cost, total_calls, by_model. Filter handles Windows backslashes via _canonicalize_session_set_path() and accepts basename matches (relying on the repo convention that session-set basenames are unique under docs/session-sets/<unique-slug>). Honors AI_ROUTER_METRICS_PATH env override so tests can redirect without API keys. Updated get_costs() to layer routed-canonical + activity-supplemental + delta_usd + discrepancy on top of the existing activity-log shape — every old top-level key (total_calls, total_cost, by_model, sessions_completed, sessions_remaining) preserved.

Updated print_cost_report() to print both totals side-by-side with the spec-required labels ('Routed-model spend (canonical):' / 'Activity-log adjustments (supplemental):'), warn on |delta| > $0.01 with direction-labeled WARNING (more vs less), surface missing metrics file or activity log to the operator, and added format='text'|'json' parameter. JSON output rounds floats to 6 dp and uses sort_keys=True for stable diffs.

Wrote tests/test_cost_report.py with 23 tests across TestMatchesSessionSet, TestGetCosts (matching, mismatched, threshold edges, missing metrics, missing activity-log, both missing, cross-set filter, Windows-path canonicalization, by-model aggregation), TestPrintCostReportText (both totals, warning directions, missing-file note), TestPrintCostReportJson (parseable, invalid format raises), and TestIntegration (end-to-end round trip).

Cross-provider verification routed to gemini-pro per user instruction. Result: ISSUES_FOUND. (1 Major) basename matching could risk cross-set contamination; (2 Minor) activity_log_present always True because SessionLog constructor creates the missing file. See session-reviews/session-001.md for full review.

Addressed both findings. Finding 1: documented the basename-uniqueness assumption in _matches_session_set rather than removing basename matching (removal would break the production flow where records carry relative paths but consumers may pass absolute paths); the verifier's strict-fix recommendation was rejected with a documented rationale in session-reviews/session-001.md. Finding 2: probe os.path.isfile(activity_path) BEFORE constructing SessionLog so the missing-log diagnostic state is correctly surfaced. Added two regression tests (test_activity_log_present_false_when_file_absent, test_activity_log_present_true_when_file_exists).
Full ai-router test suite: 479 passed (was 477; +2 regression tests). No regressions.
Wrote session-reviews/session-001.md with the verifier's full review, both findings, and the resolution + rationale for each.
